source_plan_ref: operator working note outside the repository: aoa-evals-strategic-understanding-notes.md

requirements_review.claim_limit: The repo now routes proof as a positive owner operation with explicit stop-lines. This does not make negative boundary language the architecture and does not complete the long goal.
Durable re-entry, steering, working artifacts, and proof-loop review are now routeable from files. This does not create hidden schedules, hidden verdicts, or autonomous runtime authority.
Sibling and center lessons are adapted as proof-boundary routes. This does not absorb sibling owner truth or copy another repo literally.
Authority classes, sibling refs, and source-derived-candidate-receipt splits are mapped before further movement. This does not make the map stronger than the source surfaces.
The root design trio is present and routeable. It does not replace bundle-local proof meaning, architecture depth, or eval philosophy.
Durable decisions now explain topology, validation, route, and report choices. They remain rationale, not proof source authority.
Direction, release narrative, obligation records, quest lifecycle, and generated readers are separated. Quests still do not become eval bundles or verdict authority.
The convex topology criterion is now carried by explicit source, derived, candidate, receipt, sibling, legacy, and mechanic classes. It does not authorize arbitrary file movement.
Mechanics packages route real proof operations, current parent direction, and stronger owner splits. They do not move source bundles or become empty taxonomy.
Legacy and accepted-input names are preserved as provenance and routing compatibility. They do not steer active topology by historical inertia.

Validator and tests now constrain named design, topology, quest, mechanics, generated route residue, report, receipt, release, and closeout invariants. Green checks do not prove broader truth outside those invariants.
The active proof loop is locally usable through smoke, bundle-local report, report index, and dry receipt intake. It still publishes no live receipt and promotes no bundle.
Runtime and machine evidence are candidate intake surfaces for bundle-local review. This audit does not mutate abyss-stack, run live machine integration, or accept runtime verdict authority.
The maintained Spark lane is under .agents and route-card covered. It does not widen proof claims or make .agents a doctrine center.

The local release-prep handoff is reviewable after broad local gates. It is not a tag, GitHub Release, PR approval, GitHub Repo Validation, or goal completion; PR and Repo Validation remain required landing evidence for this operator route.
Known traps are audited into route, validation, objective-completion boundaries, and landing boundaries. This report is a handoff audit, not a completion ceremony.

trap_review.mitigation: The working note was turned into repo-local design, decision, mechanics, report, quest, and validator surfaces rather than left as private memory.
Root design routes to bundle-local source truth and depth docs instead of replacing them.
Decision notes are tied to concrete topology, validation, mechanics, report, and route changes.
Questbook, quest source records, lifecycle states, generated readers, roadmap, and eval verdict meaning are separated.
Mechanic packages exist only for named proof operations with validators and stronger-owner splits.
Sibling refs are local compatibility evidence with canary coverage, not cross-repo mutation or authority transfer.
Runtime evidence is candidate-only and does not pull abyss-stack into aoa-evals as a live owner.
Route docs now name owned operations, inputs, outputs, and handoffs before stop-lines.
Legacy names are mapped as provenance bridges, accepted inputs, generated projections, or candidate-only vocabulary.
Validator and tests check semantic statuses, generated route residue, open publication boundaries, required commands, and report subordination.
Release readiness is separated from actual tag and release publication; PR approval and GitHub Repo Validation remain landing requirements, not readiness claims.
The first proof-loop path ends in bundle-local report and dry receipt intake only, with no live publication.

verification_snapshot.claim_limit: Focused strategic closeout tests constrained this handoff report after it was added.
Focused generated-route residue tests constrain stale generated/readout references without over-policing part-local generated sibling paths.
Focused active mechanic route residue tests constrain authored route-card paths without blocking root route cards or same-part local references.
Focused mechanic payload route residue tests constrain active fixtures, schemas, manifests, scripts, tests, and hooks without blocking part-local or repo-qualified sibling refs.
Focused root authored route residue tests constrain root-facing guidance paths without blocking root route cards, historical context, or active bundle and mechanic routes.
Focused decision route residue tests constrain current-looking decision paths without erasing historical or provenance context.
Focused repo config route residue tests constrain executable config paths without erasing historical or provenance context.
Focused source bundle route residue tests constrain proof-object route paths without moving bundle meaning into mechanics.
Focused mechanic part README tests constrain generic part routing and contract headings; they do not prove any part is proof-complete.
Focused mechanic part payload inventory tests constrain part-local payload visibility and reject empty or unknown payload directories; they do not prove any part is proof-complete.

Focused mechanic part validation command tests constrain repo-relative reachable validation paths and payload coverage anchors; they do not execute every part command or prove any part is proof-complete.
Focused mechanic PARTS index synchronization tests constrain local parent part maps; they do not turn cross-parent stop-line references into local ownership.
Focused mechanic legacy single-bridge tests constrain active surfaces to route archive detail through provenance; they do not prove every archived source has been distilled.
Focused mechanic provenance bridge-posture tests constrain PROVENANCE.md as a bridge, not an active route; they do not make legacy archive detail active topology.
Focused legacy naming single-bridge tests constrain docs/architecture/LEGACY_NAMING.md wording; they do not prove every archived source has been distilled.
Focused active legacy parent wording tests constrain old parent-form vocabulary in active route wording without blocking schema filename compatibility or explicit provenance context.
Focused mechanic provenance tests constrain active-first provenance routing through index, distillation log, and raw-lineage boundary; they do not make legacy active topology.
Focused mechanic parent direction tests constrain parent DIRECTION surfaces and parent entry routes; they do not create new mechanics or strengthen proof claims.
Focused mechanic evidence dimension tests constrain the parent evidence ledger; they do not make the ledger source proof authority.
Focused mechanic root-district reconnaissance tests constrain the root-district ledger; they do not move payloads or make the ledger source proof authority.
Focused root-authored surface classification tests constrain residual root docs, scripts, and tests; they do not move payloads or make root surfaces mechanic-owned.
Validates repository invariants for the current local checkout; it does not replace requirement-by-requirement objective audit or the requested GitHub landing route.

Local release audit passed; it is not a tag, GitHub Release, PR approval, or proof that eval claims grew stronger.

claim_limit: This strategic closeout audit states local strategic refactor handoff evidence while the current mechanics objective audit and requested GitHub landing route remain open. It does not mark the goal complete before the current objective audit, commit, push, PR, GitHub Repo Validation, merge, fast-forward main, and clean worktree; does not treat PR or GitHub landing alone as objective completion; does not publish a release; does not create a tag; does not publish a GitHub Release; does not publish an eval result receipt; does not promote any bundle; does not accept runtime evidence; and does not mutate sibling repos.
